title: podman exposes udp port on tcp-only image
body:
podman with `--publish-all` exposes udp even if image is tcp-only:
```
# cat >Dockerfile
FROM centos
EXPOSE 8080      ! default is tcp, but you can append '/tcp' if you like; no diff
^D
# img=$(podman build --quiet .)
# cid=$(podman run -d --publish-all $img sleep 60)
# podman port $cid
8080/udp -> 0.0.0.0:35365
8080/tcp -> 0.0.0.0:35365
```
It doesn't seem to be buildah's fault:
```
# podman inspect $img|grep -2 8080
        "ContainerConfig": {
            "ExposedPorts": {
                "8080/tcp": {}
            },
            "Cmd": [
```
RPMs:
```
buildah-0.16-1524754276.git83f4830.fc27.x86_64
podman-0.5.2-1525700809.gitfaa8c3e.fc27.x86_64
```

comments.body:
We talked a little about this on IRC.  @rhatdan , if you recall, when we started enabling the network stack I asked about whether we need to distinguish between the tcp and udp protocols.  At that time, we jointly made the decision to treat them both as one.  This is obviously a departure from what other container runtimes do.

Fixing the example that @edsantiago provides may actually be doable ... but if this has to be be extended into -p and friends, it will be a significant amount of work.
@edsantiago WDYT?  Is this a real issue?

The worst (and least unlikely) scenario I can think of where this matters is 53: I can envision a container running BIND, where the creator wants to expose 53/udp but does not want to allow AXFR on 53/tcp, and they do so by filtering instead of by configuration. It would tick me off if I were to discover that my container was not running as I had crafted it. But I'm not the sort of person who would do that.

IMO this isn't worth five days' effort but if it's easy to do a subset, it's worth considering.

Disclaimer: there are almost certainly other cases I haven't thought of.
Ok lets leave this as an issue, but put it to the bottom of the queue.  Or if some contributor wants to take it on, go for it.
@baude Is this something we should have an intern look at?

I'd prefer to fix it given the history of this code ... the larger question is if we are going to still assume -P 80 for example is both udp and tcp.  and if we are going to start respecting -P 80/tcp
Yes I think -P 80 means both and then you have to give specific for tcp and udp.

this issue seems still persist. @baude if you are busy, may I try to fix this?

```
$ cat Dockerfile.builah 
FROM docker.io/library/centos:latest
EXPOSE 8080

$ cid=$(sudo podman run -d -p 6061/tcp $img sleep 60)

$ sudo podman port $cid                              
6061/udp -> 0.0.0.0:34753
6061/tcp -> 0.0.0.0:34753

```
@kunalkushwaha I don't think anyone is actively working on this issue, so feel free
Yes, feel free
@kunalkushwaha We would love to have you work on this.  Thanks.
Thanks, I will start looking into this.

Just to review, I have a put down the current and expected behaviour of  `-p` and `-P|--publish-all` output of ports. 

|   Options	|  Now  	|  Expected 	| 
|---	|---	|---	|
| `-p 80`   	|   `80/tcp`, `80/udp`	|  `80/tcp`  	|
| `-p 80/tcp`  	|  `80/tcp`, `80/udp` 	| `80/tcp`  	|
|   `-p 80/udp`	| `80/tcp`, `80/udp`  	|  `80/udp` 	|
|   `-P` with `80` in image 	| `80/tcp`, `80/udp`  	| `80/tcp`, `80/udp`*	|
|   `-P` with `80/udp` in image	| `80/tcp`, `80/udp`  	|  `80/udp` 	|

*_In other runtimes like docker ,here `80/tcp` is exposed_

Please let me  know, if any changes in expected behaviour is required.
LGTM
When the protocol is not stated, are both udp and tcp ports supposed to be used?
Per docker it's just TCP if not specified.
ok, works for me!
Reference: [EXPOSE](https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#expose) is documented as explicitly defaulting to tcp. To my surprise, I've been unable to find a definitive reference for `-p`.

While debugging, I found, in case of no proto defined for exposed ports in Image(Dockerfile), the `tcp` is value we get after Image is parsed as per  `ociV1 Spec`
- the `tcp`  value is returned from function [`ociv1Image()`](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/libpod/image/image.go#L787) 

Also I cross checked the [OCI specs](https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/blob/master/config.md#properties), and it states
 > __ExposedPorts object, OPTIONAL__
> A set of ports to expose from a container running this image. Its keys can be in the format of: port/tcp, port/udp, port with __the default protocol being tcp if not specified.__

I feel we should go as per specs i.e. default `tcp` port exposed in case of no proto  specified, so no data is lost in translation from OCI image to container.
So updated table of current vs expected will be 

|   Options	|  Now  	|  Expected 	| 
|---	|---	|---	|
| `-p 80`   	|   `80/tcp`, `80/udp`	|  `80/tcp`  	|
| `-p 80/tcp`  	|  `80/tcp`, `80/udp` 	| `80/tcp`  	|
|   `-p 80/udp`	| `80/tcp`, `80/udp`  	|  `80/udp` 	|
|   `-P` with `80` in image 	| `80/tcp`, `80/udp`  	| `80/tcp`|
|   `-P` with `80/udp` in image	| `80/tcp`, `80/udp`  	|  `80/udp` 	|

Please let me know WDYT
SGTM
This issue can be closed now.
